checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain any fabricated paragraphs or sentences. The headings and body text match the content found in the source PDF. The duplicated sections (PE-15, PE-16, PE-17) are repetitions of existing source content rather than hallucinations.
No phone numbers, email addresses, or URLs were found in the output that are not in the source. The source document itself lacks specific contact details like phone numbers or email addresses.

All primary policy sections (Scope, Material Superseded, Responsibilities, and control sections PE-1 through PE-20) are present in the output. No entire pages or logical document sections were omitted.

The 'Roles and Responsibilities' table in the output correctly contains 4 rows (1 header and 3 data rows), matching the source PDF exactly. The 'Third Parties Responsibility' table is also correctly represented.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The core text of each section is preserved. While some sections are duplicated across different parts of the JSON structure, the complete text for those sections exists within the overall output.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The output fails to include critical document metadata found in the header of every page of the source PDF. Specifically, the Document Number (SCIO-SEC-313), the Version Number (4), the Effective Date (01/29/2018), and the Review Date (03/26/2025) are entirely missing from the body and metadata of the converted document. These are key informational identifiers for a government policy.
checks.b_evidence_source: Document No. SCIO-SEC-313, Effective Date 01/29/2018, Review Date 03/26/2025, Version 4
checks.b_evidence_output: The fields for Document No, Effective Date, Review Date, and Version are absent from the JSON elements and metadata.

checks.a_analysis: The output exhibits significant content duplication. The text for sections PE-15 (Water Damage Protection), PE-16 (Delivery and Removal), and PE-17 (Alternate Work Site) appears at the end of section 1 (elements 38-49) and is then repeated in dedicated subsequent sections 2, 3, and 4. This is a clear structural defect where content is segments and repeated redundantly.
checks.b_evidence_source: The sections PE-15, PE-16, and PE-17 appear exactly once on page 11 and continue on page 12 of the source document.
checks.b_evidence_output: Section PE-15 text appears in Section 1 (starting at element index 38) and is duplicated in Section 2. Section PE-16 text appears in Section 1 (element index 42) and is duplicated in Section 3.

checks.a_analysis: The heading hierarchy begins with an H2 for the document title ('Physical and Environmental Protection Policy') and major sections like 'Scope'. According to accessibility standards, the document title should be H1, and major sections should be H2. Starting the hierarchy at H2 without an H1 is a break in structural hierarchy.
checks.b_evidence_source: 'Physical and Environmental Protection Policy' is clearly the top-level document title.

checks.a_analysis: The main Responsibilities table from page 1 and the Third Parties callout box from page 2 are converted correctly into table structures with appropriate headers and cell data. No structural corruption was observed.

The general flow of information follows the document's sequence. While duplication and list issues exist, the reading order itself is not fundamentally inverted or logically broken (e.g., conclusions before introductions).

The conversion of complex lists is inconsistent. In section PE-17 (Section 4), the sub-items 'ii' through 'vi' belonging to item 'd' (found on source page 12) are completely missing from the list structure in the output. Additionally, the list in PE-2 from page 3 (items i-viii) is orphaned into a separate list object rather than being properly nested or continued as a single list structure, losing the connection to the preceding items on page 2.
checks.b_evidence_source: PE-17 item 'd' contains a nested list with items i, ii, iii, iv, v, vi (pages 11-12).
checks.b_evidence_output: In Section 4, the list for PE-17 contains item 'd' which has a 'nested_list' containing only sub-item 'i'. Items 'ii' through 'vi' are not present.

checks.a_analysis: There are no links in the source PDF, and no fabricated links were found in the output. NIST references are cited as text, as expected.

checks.a_analysis: The converted HTML document follows the same logical organization as the source PDF. The sequence of headers, policy sections (PE-1 through PE-20), and tables matches the source. Although the layout has changed from a fixed-page PDF format with specific headers/footers to a continuous single-column responsive HTML layout, the visual hierarchy and relationship between sections remain consistent. A user can easily locate information in the HTML that corresponds to its location in the PDF.

The tables present in the source PDF (e.g., the 'Roles and Responsibilities' table on page 1 and the 'Third Parties' table on page 2) are correctly rendered in the HTML. They maintain a tabular structure with distinct headers and cells, making the data easily readable and understandable for a sighted user.

The meaningful image from the source PDF, the Great Seal of the State of North Carolina logo found in the header of every page, is present and correctly rendered in the HTML output. It appears at the top of the document and is repeated throughout, reflecting its presence on each page of the original source.

There is significant visual duplication of body content in the rendered HTML. The sections PE-15 (Water Damage Protection), PE-16 (Delivery and Removal), and the first part of PE-17 (Alternate Work Site) that appear on page 11 of the PDF are rendered once in the HTML and then immediately repeated. The first iteration of PE-17 ends prematurely at item 'i' (matching the end of PDF page 11), followed by the complete block for PE-15, PE-16, and the full PE-17 (items a through e). This is a genuine duplication of body content rather than a navigation element.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 11 contains sections PE-15, PE-16, and the beginning of PE-17 (items a-d.i).
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows these sections appearing twice in the body. The first instance ends after 'PE-17... i. Sufficient physical access controls...', and is immediately followed by a second rendering starting again with 'PE-15—Water Damage Protection'.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections PE-15, PE-16, and PE-17, corresponding to the transition from PDF page 11 to page 12.
